expectations.text: workflow.md validates cleanly via `markflow init` (exit 0, no parse/validation errors)
File contains an `# Inputs` section declaring a `city` parameter
`# Flow` section present with exactly one fenced mermaid block
expectations.evidence: Lines 15-20: flowchart TD with fetch-weather, recommend, done, error

expectations.text: `# Steps` section present; every node referenced in the flow has a matching `## <id>` heading and every `## <id>` heading appears in the flow
expectations.evidence: All four nodes (fetch-weather, recommend, done, error) have ## headings; all ## headings referenced

expectations.text: Includes at least one script step (```bash / ```sh / ```python / ```js) that performs the weather fetch
expectations.evidence: ## fetch-weather uses ```bash with curl to wttr.in

expectations.text: Includes at least one agent step (plain prose under a `##` heading, no fenced code block body) for the dog-walk recommendation
expectations.evidence: ## recommend is plain prose with a config block; no body code fence

expectations.text: The agent step references the city via Liquid templating (e.g., `{{ city }}` or `{{ GLOBAL.* }}` populated from the fetch step) — not hard-coded
No use of banned patterns: `local`/`global` keys nested inside `RESULT`, unpaired `max:N`/`:max` edges, or unknown code-block language
expectations.evidence: No RESULT nesting, no retry edges, only bash code blocks
